mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already below recent levels, discount rates ease and equity valuation multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed if rate cuts coincide with improving inflation or labor data rather than recessionary collapse.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in most periods where FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL is stable and inflation is not accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent baseline indicates weakening household income and demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp versus the prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears after 2008-style stress periods are excluded.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in retail sales proxy RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after RSAFS strength, or if the relation reverses when consumer spending data are simultaneously weakening.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS indicates improving real output and labor utilization, which reduces recession odds and lifts equity returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when production rises only through inventory restocking without labor gains.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with jointly rising INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is positive only when one proxy improves but not both.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing demand contraction, tighter financial conditions, and weaker cyclical growth, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if housing-threshold breakdowns are followed by positive SPX returns as often as negative returns, or if the effect is absent when rates are not rising.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy becomes tighter and valuation pressure increases, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is rising faster than policy can respond.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains negative even when CPIAUCSL is decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market improvement, strengthening earnings visibility and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not valid if payroll gains are accompanied by a rising unemployment rate.
failure_condition: Reject if jointly improving payrolls and unemployment do not precede higher 60-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur equally when UNRATE is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, declining GDPC1 identifies contracting final demand and rising earnings risk, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative. This signal is activated only when the economy is already shrinking, not during normal slowdowns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is negative on a quarter-over-quarter annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 episodes are not followed by below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is positive during contraction periods more often than negative.
